name: World History
description: Global events, empires, and turning points

questions.q: In what year did the Berlin Wall fall?

source.notes: November 9, 1989. Reunified Germany in October 1990.

questions.q: Who was the last Tsar (Emperor) of Russia?

source.notes: Reigned 1894-1917. Executed with his family by Bolsheviks in 1918.

questions.q: The Marshall Plan was a US initiative to rebuild what region after WWII?

source.notes: 1948-1952. $13 billion ($150B in today's money). Named after Secretary of State George Marshall.

questions.q: What was the code name for the WWII secret project that developed the atomic bomb?

source.notes: Led by J. Robert Oppenheimer. Produced bombs dropped on Hiroshima and Nagasaki.

questions.q: Which Duke defeated Napoleon at the Battle of Waterloo in 1815?

source.notes: Arthur Wellesley. Later British Prime Minister. The 'Wellington boot' named after him.

questions.q: In what year did Fidel Castro take power in Cuba?

source.notes: Overthrew Batista January 1959. Led Cuba until 2008.

questions.q: Who was the first female Prime Minister in the world?

source.notes: Bandaranaike of Ceylon (now Sri Lanka). Served 1960-1965. First woman head of government in modern history.

questions.q: In 1989, which Berlin landmark reopened after the fall of the Wall?

source.notes: Had stood in no man's land during partition. Reopened December 22, 1989.

questions.q: Which invading people's raids prompted the original construction of the Great Wall of China?

source.notes: Built to defend against northern nomadic peoples, particularly Xiongnu (related to Mongols and Huns).

questions.q: The Cuban Missile Crisis took place in what year?

source.notes: October 1962. 13-day standoff between US and USSR — closest to nuclear war.

questions.q: Which war was fought between North and South Korea, with UN and Chinese forces involved?

source.notes: 1950-1953. Armistice signed but no peace treaty — technically still ongoing.

questions.q: Who was the first emperor of ancient Rome, succeeding Julius Caesar?

source.notes: Reigned 27 BC - AD 14. Adopted grand-nephew of Julius Caesar. Originally named Octavian.

questions.q: Which disease, also called the 'Black Death,' killed an estimated 25-50M people in 14th-century Europe?

source.notes: ~1347-1351 peak. Spread by fleas on rats. Killed 30-60% of Europe's population.

questions.q: Mahatma Gandhi led India to independence from which country in 1947?

source.notes: Known for nonviolent resistance. Assassinated January 30, 1948.

questions.q: In which South African city did Nelson Mandela serve 18 of his 27 years in prison?

source.notes: Released 1990. Became South Africa's first black president in 1994.

questions.q: The Russian Revolution began in what year?

source.notes: February and October Revolutions in 1917. Led to overthrow of Tsar Nicholas II and rise of the Bolsheviks.

questions.q: Which empire did Hernán Cortés conquer in the early 1500s?

source.notes: Defeated Aztec emperor Moctezuma II by 1521. Francisco Pizarro conquered the Inca in the 1530s.

questions.q: What 1215 document is considered foundational to modern Western democracy?

source.notes: Signed by King John at Runnymede. Limited royal power. Influenced Common Law and the US Constitution.

questions.q: The 1980 Solidarity movement against communism began in shipyards in which country?

source.notes: Gdańsk shipyards. Led by Lech Wałęsa. Helped bring down communism in Poland in 1989.

questions.q: Which ancient Egyptian female pharaoh is famous for ruling around 51-30 BC?

source.notes: Last active ruler of the Ptolemaic Kingdom. Had relationships with Julius Caesar and Mark Antony.
